content.text: Circuit Tracks can send and receive MIDI, making it a powerful hub for your hardware setup. Connect synths, drum machines, and DAWs using its full-size MIDI ports or USB.
Circuit Tracks features full-size 5-pin DIN MIDI ports (In, Out, and Thru) plus USB-C for MIDI connectivity. No adapters required.

content.title: Full MIDI Implementation
content.text: Circuit Tracks has dedicated 5-pin DIN MIDI In, Out, and Thru ports. The Thru port passes incoming MIDI to other devices for easy daisy-chaining without needing a separate MIDI thru box.

In addition to the synth and sample tracks, Circuit Tracks has two dedicated MIDI tracks designed specifically for sequencing external hardware. These tracks don't produce internal sound - they only output MIDI data.

items: Velocity and gate length per step
Configurable MIDI channel per track (via Components)
CC automation using the macro knobs
Polyphonic note entry (up to 6 notes per step)

content.text: Each track transmits and receives on its own MIDI channel:
All drum tracks share channel 10 (standard for drums) but use different note numbers. MIDI track channels can be configured in Novation Components.
To control an external synth with Circuit Tracks:

items: Connect MIDI Out to your synth's MIDI In using a standard 5-pin DIN cable
Set your synth to receive on the matching channel
Select a synth track or MIDI track and play
For MIDI tracks only (no internal sound): Use MIDI Track 1 or 2
To mute internal sound: Set the internal track's mixer level to zero

content.text: Circuit Tracks sends MIDI clock when playing, keeping external gear in sync. It can also receive clock from an external source to act as a follower in your setup.
Use Novation Components to configure clock settings - choose whether to send, receive, or ignore MIDI clock. You can also configure the clock output to only send on play or continuously.
When connected to a computer via USB-C, Circuit Tracks appears as a class-compliant MIDI device in your DAW. No drivers required. This enables:

items: Playing soft synths from Circuit Tracks
Using Circuit Tracks as a MIDI controller
Accessing Novation Components for deep editing

content.text: Circuit Tracks sends and receives MIDI CC for various parameters. The eight macro knobs send CC messages that can be mapped in your DAW or to parameters on external synths. CC values are also recorded when step-recording with parameter locks.
